name: sql_validation_test_generation
objective: Generate exactly one executable Oracle SELECT statement that compares source SQL row counts and target SQL row counts for each bind case.
rules: [Common output rules]
Use Oracle 19c compatible SQL only.
Return exactly one executable Oracle SELECT statement.
The returned SQL must output only CASE_NO, FROM_COUNT, and TO_COUNT columns.
Use at most 3 validation cases. If bind_set_json has more than 3 items, use only the first 3.
Create one validation row per selected bind_set_json item and connect multiple rows with UNION ALL.
Every SELECT block connected by UNION ALL must end with FROM DUAL, even when the SELECT list only contains scalar subqueries.
Use this shape for each case: SELECT <case_number> AS CASE_NO, (<source_count_query>) AS FROM_COUNT, (<target_count_query>) AS TO_COUNT FROM DUAL.
The final validation SQL must not contain unresolved placeholders such as #{{param}}, ${{param}}, :param, ?, {{{{param}}}}, or ${{...}}.
Do not output comments, markdown, JSON, explanations, PL/SQL, multiple SQL statements, or a trailing semicolon.
[COUNT wrapping rules]
source_sql and target_sql are already the SQL statements being compared. Do not rewrite their meaning or create new SQL from mapping rules.
Preserve each SQL as much as possible and wrap it as SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (<source_sql>) S and SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (<target_sql>) T.
During test SQL generation, only perform bind value substitution, MyBatis dynamic tag handling, and ORDER BY removal when ORDER BY is unnecessary or can cause Oracle COUNT errors.
Use only source_schema and target_schema for schema decisions. Do not add a schema again to already-qualified tables.
Do not reconstruct columns, tables, joins, or SELECT lists from mapping rules. Do not infer or add objects not present in source_sql or target_sql.
If comparison_mode is TARGET_TO_TARGET, treat both source_sql and target_sql as TO-BE SQL using target_schema.
Preserve CTEs, inline views, and virtual tables defined inside the original SQL. Do not schema-qualify CTE names, inline-view aliases, subquery aliases, or DUAL.
Do not explain or return the original source_sql or target_sql by itself. Return only the final validation SELECT.
[Dynamic tag rules]
In normal attempts, replace MyBatis placeholders in source_sql and target_sql with executable Oracle literals from the current bind case.

In normal attempts, evaluate MyBatis dynamic tags (<if>, <choose>, <when>, <otherwise>, <where>, <trim>, <foreach>) using the current bind case values. No MyBatis tags may remain in the final executable SQL.
After removing <where> and <trim>, clean up WHERE/AND/OR syntax so the SQL remains valid.
For foreach, if the bind case value is an array, expand it into IN (...) or an equivalent Oracle row source. If no value exists, treat that dynamic condition as inactive.
[Final retry dynamic bypass rules]
If last_error contains FINAL_RETRY_MODE=ON or attempt=3/3, this section overrides the normal dynamic tag rules.

On the final retry, do not evaluate MyBatis dynamic tag conditions. Simplify source_sql and target_sql as if dynamic tags and their inner SQL fragments were removed, then wrap the simplified SQL with COUNT.
On the final retry, do not generate placeholder substitutions, CASE, EXISTS, extra joins, or extra subqueries that are needed only for dynamic tag condition evaluation.
On the final retry, parameters used only inside dynamic tag fragments must not be used. Those dynamic SQL fragments must not appear in TEST_SQL.
On the final retry, keep static SQL and static joins outside dynamic tags, and substitute only static placeholders with bind case values.
[Error recovery rules]
If last_error is present, prioritize fixing that previous error.
Do not use ORDER BY in validation SQL. Row count comparison does not need ordering, and ORDER BY inside subqueries, inline views, or CTEs can cause Oracle errors.
If ORA-00907 occurred, remove ORDER BY clauses from COUNT-based validation SQL and all inner subqueries.
If a scalar subquery in SELECT can return multiple rows because of GROUP BY, remove GROUP BY so it returns one row or change the structure to a JOIN-based query.
If parenthesis errors repeat, minimize outer parentheses and ensure each COUNT subquery has the form SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (<valid_select>) alias.
user_instruction: Generate exactly one executable Oracle validation SELECT statement. source_sql and target_sql are already the comparison targets; do not rewrite them. Wrap each SQL as a COUNT subquery and compare FROM_COUNT with TO_COUNT. In normal attempts, only substitute bind values, evaluate MyBatis dynamic tags, and remove ORDER BY. In final retry mode (FINAL_RETRY_MODE=ON or attempt=3/3), do not evaluate MyBatis dynamic tag condition logic; simplify the SQL as if dynamic tags and their inner SQL fragments were removed, so TEST_SQL does not show dynamic-tag-only conditions or joins. Return CASE_NO, FROM_COUNT, and TO_COUNT, use at most 3 cases, ensure every UNION ALL SELECT block ends with FROM DUAL, and do not add a trailing semicolon.
